---
title: "Infer cell-cell communication"
description: "Ligand-receptor interaction inference between cell types (LIANA)."
categories: [openpipeline]
order: 60
---

[`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline}<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>

Cell-cell communication analysis infers which cell types signal to each other by
scoring **ligand-receptor** pairs: a source cell type expressing a ligand and a
target expressing its receptor. OpenPipeline wraps
[LIANA](https://liana-py.readthedocs.io/), which runs several scoring methods and
returns a consensus ranking, so results don't hinge on one method's assumptions.

## Options

| Component | Use when |
|---|---|
| [`interpret/lianapy`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/interpret/lianapy.qmd) | Rank ligand-receptor interactions between annotated cell types |

## Example: LIANA

For example, on an [annotated](annotate.qmd) object with cell-type labels in
`.obs["cell_type"]`:

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline.git \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/interpret/lianapy/main.nf \
  --input results/annotated.h5mu \
  --modality rna \
  --groupby cell_type \
  --output communication.h5mu \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Key choices

- **`--groupby`**: the `.obs` cell-type column, it defines the sender and
  receiver groups, so [annotation](annotate.qmd) must run first.
- **`--de_method`**, **`--resource_name`**, and **`--aggregate_method`** select
  the scoring method, the ligand-receptor resource, and how methods are combined,
  see the reference.

## Output

A table of ranked ligand-receptor interactions between cell-type pairs, written
into the object.
